posts.title: Best Things I Read This Week - 2
posts.description: A weekly roundup on boredom, teaching, courage, compounding, and why hard problems are worth choosing.
posts.excerpt: Notes on boredom as a filter, learning by teaching, courage in decisions, and why difficult work creates real advantage.

posts.title: Lessons from Pavel Durov on Freedom, Discipline, and Purpose
posts.description: Reflections on freedom, self-control, physical discipline, and building a principled life from Pavel Durov's worldview.
posts.excerpt: A deep reflection on freedom, fear, discipline, physical training, and protecting attention in a world built to distract you.

posts.title: Best Thing I Read This Week
posts.description: Quick notes on Naval's formulas for life, Sam Altman on luck, Munger on learning, and Brady on consistency.
posts.excerpt: A shorter weekly note covering Naval's formulas, Sam Altman on luck, Charlie Munger on learning, and Tom Brady on consistency.

posts.title: The Art of Start
posts.description: A first-principles guide to beginning hard things with clear goals, smart work, and patient repetition.
posts.excerpt: A practical essay on first-principles thinking, defining goals well, and using systems instead of waiting for perfect confidence.

posts.title: Less is More
posts.description: Why abundance can destroy attention, and why a life built on fewer wants often feels more free.
posts.excerpt: A short reflection on excess, distraction, and why subtraction often creates more freedom than accumulation.

posts.title: Clear Thinking: Escaping the Defaults
posts.description: A practical summary of Shane Parrish's core idea: the antidote to reactive stupidity is cultivated strength.
posts.excerpt: A summary of the four defaults that trap judgment and the habits that help us think more clearly under pressure.

posts.title: Is Social Media the New Religion?
posts.description: A meditation on algorithms, influence, belonging, and the spiritual power social platforms now hold over attention.
posts.excerpt: An essay on how platforms mimic religion through ritual, belonging, incentives, and the economics of attention.

posts.title: Are Humans Getting Stupider?
posts.description: A political and cultural reflection on attention, algorithms, leadership quality, and declining public judgment.
posts.excerpt: A reflection on distraction, poor leadership, and the social conditions that make manipulation easier at scale.

posts.title: Science Mental Models for Life & Decisions
posts.description: Six science-based mental models, from entropy to natural selection, that help with everyday decisions.
posts.excerpt: A guide to entropy, feedback loops, limiting factors, inertia, and other science patterns that show up in life and work.

posts.title: Math Mental Models for Life & Decisions
posts.description: Compounding, power laws, base rates, expected value, and other math ideas that improve real-world judgment.
posts.excerpt: A practical tour of compounding, power laws, base rates, expected value, and sensitivity analysis for better choices.
